EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Dry fuels along with windy conditions help spread the human caused Geronimo Wildfire in Cochise County.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Geronimo wildfire burned nearly 2500 acres of mainly grass near Sunizona in Cochise County. There were about 8 small structures burned including two vehicles.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A second storm in two days brought record amounts of snow to portions of northern Arizona on February 9th. The Arizona Department of Public Safety reported hundreds of vehicle collisions across northern Arizona.  One DPS commander said that it was the worst snowstorm that he had to deal with in northern Arizona.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A winter storm caused a 12 mile traffic back up on westbound I-40 near Seligman.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A second storm in two days brought record amounts of snow to portions of northern Arizona on February 9th. The Arizona Department of Public Safety reported hundreds of vehicle collisions across northern Arizona.  One DPS commander said that it was the worst snowstorm that he had to deal with in northern Arizona.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Six inches of snow fell in Payson and Star Valley. Fifteen inches of snow fell in Tonto Village.  Highways 87, 260, and 60 were closed because road crews could not keep up with the heavy snow. Schools were closed and trash pick up was canceled. A dozen garbage trucks were used to plow roads instead. School buses couldn't even get out of the parking area on the morning of February 10th.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Winds increased to 30 to 45 mph by late Friday afternoon. Isolated wind gusts over 50 mph were reported in the Globe area.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Winds reached up to 46 mph at Deer Valley airport. At least one large tree was toppled by the strong winds.

CZ_NAME: LITTLE COLORADO RIVER VALLEY IN APACHE COUNTY
EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong cold front brought very strong and gusty winds to northern Arizona on March 22, 2009.  The winds locally caused damage to buildings, power outages, and near zero visibility in blowing dust.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The ASOS at the St Johns Airport reported a peak wind gust of 66 MPH.  The Petrified Forest National Park reported a peak wind gust of 58 MPH.

CZ_NAME: LITTLE COLORADO RIVER VALLEY IN COCONINO COUNTY
EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong cold front brought very strong and gusty winds to northern Arizona on March 22, 2009.  The winds locally caused damage to buildings, power outages, and near zero visibility in blowing dust.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: An ADOT anemometer at Leupp Road and I-40 reported a peak wind gust of 67 MPH. The Police Department in Tuba City reported very strong winds with periods of 1/4 mile visibility in blowing dust. A spotter in Leupp reported 3/4 to 1 mile in spotty blowing dust.  Blowing dust along Highway 89 between Gap and Cameron reduced the visibility to 30 feet in places. A spotter at Meteor Crater reported about 1/2 mile visibility on I-40.

CZ_NAME: LITTLE COLORADO RIVER VALLEY IN NAVAJO COUNTY
EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong cold front brought very strong and gusty winds to northern Arizona on March 22, 2009.  The winds locally caused damage to buildings, power outages, and near zero visibility in blowing dust.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The ASOS at the Winslow Airport reported a wind gust to 64 MPH. A spotter in Winslow reported that the wind knocked down an elm tree.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A prolonged episode of gusty southwest winds produced isolated high wind reports in southeast California, southern Nevada, and northwest Arizona.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A brief wind gust 12 miles ESE of Cedar Hills damaged the awning of a mobile home and knocked over some feed buckets.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A prolonged episode of gusty southwest winds produced isolated high wind reports in southeast California, southern Nevada, and northwest Arizona.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: This gust occurred at the Bullhead City Airport (KIFP).

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Near record high temperatures were observed across South Central Arizona.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Several heat-related illnesses were reported in Glendale as bright sunshine and near record temperatures were reported during a baseball game.  The official high temperature in Phoenix on Sunday was 86 degrees, and the record high was 89 degrees.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong upper level low moving across Southeast Arizona resulted in isolated thunderstorms that produced cloud to ground lightning.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A residence on the Northwest side of Tucson was struck by lightning. A cinder block retaining wall suffered the brunt of the damage.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong trough of low pressure passing through the Great Basin created gusty winds across Southeast Arizona.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Dry Lake RAWS measured a wind gust of 50 knots at 2-3 PM LST.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong trough of low pressure passing through the Great Basin created gusty winds across Southeast Arizona.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A trained spotter in Rio Rico measured a wind gust of 61 mph.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorms formed over central and eastern Maricopa County late in the evening. Strong winds were likely the cause of a large power outage in the East Valley.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Lightning was blamed on starting a house fire in Surprise.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorms rolled through the deserts near Freeman, resulting in strong winds and dense blowing dust.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Very strong winds observed along Interstate 8 at mile marker 134.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong trough of low pressure moving through the Great Basin resulted in high winds across portions of Southeast Arizona.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Douglas-Bisbee airport ASOS recorded sustained winds of 43 mph for two consecutive hours.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Scattered thunderstorms moved slowly across the south central deserts and resulted in heavy rains and locally damaging winds.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A utility trailer was flipped due to strong winds.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Scattered thunderstorms moved slowly across the south central deserts and resulted in heavy rains and locally damaging winds.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: About 25 homes on the Gila River Indian Community sustained damage with many trees uprooted. Power poles were blown down at highway 587 and Sesame Street. Four persons suffered minor injuries.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorms formed over central and eastern Maricopa County late in the evening. Strong winds were likely the cause of a large power outage in the East Valley.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Eight power poles were downed in Mesa by strong microburst winds. About 10,000 East Valley SRP customers lost power Monday night. Microburst winds were reported by a spotter in the area of Interstate 17 and Greenway Road in Phoenix. At least one tree in the East Valley was reported to be uprooted due to these storms.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: High pressure aloft remained near the Four Corners region, resulting in a continued east to northeast flow aloft over southeast Arizona. Plentiful moisture again led to scattered afternoon and evening thunderstorms. With boundary layer moisture still somewhat drier, thunderstorms were again able to produce strong to severe downburst or microburst winds.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy thunderstorm rainfall of 1 to 2 inches fell in only a 30 minute period near State Highway 86 and Three Points. Significant flooding of washes and low water crossings occurred. The Three Points fire department reported several roads were washed out and impassable in the Three Points area. The local law enforcement reported a swift water rescue was performed near the intersection of California and Worden Roads at approximately 9 PM MST.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Brief thunderstorms resulted in locally damaging winds in Tempe.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Winds damaged a small structure at a Tempe car dealership.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Microburst winds were reported northeast of Yuma as thundertorms moved through the area.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Microburst winds reached 55 mph. The blowing dust cut the visibility briefly to zero.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Several thunderstorms over Yavapai County produced large hail.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A trained spotter reported nickel sized hail.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Several thunderstorms over Yavapai County produced large hail.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Nickle sized hail was reported on the north side of Prescott.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Two lightning caused fires near the South Rim of the Grand Canyon were allowed to burn to meet management ecological and protection objectives.  The Game Reserve fire started on May 29 about nine miles southeast of the Grand Canyon Village in the Grand Canyon National Park. The Ruby started on May 25 about six miles southeast of Tusayan on the Kaibab National Forest.  The fires merged and became the Ruby Complex. The Complex burned more than 4,600 acres.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Ruby was started by lightning on May 25 about six miles southeast of Tusayan on the Kaibab National Forest.  The fires merged and became the Ruby Complex.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Two lightning caused fires near the South Rim of the Grand Canyon were allowed to burn to meet management ecological and protection objectives.  The Game Reserve fire started on May 29 about nine miles southeast of the Grand Canyon Village in the Grand Canyon National Park. The Ruby started on May 25 about six miles southeast of Tusayan on the Kaibab National Forest.  The fires merged and became the Ruby Complex. The Complex burned more than 4,600 acres.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Game Reserve fire was started by on May 29 about nine miles southeast of the Grand Canyon Village in the Grand Canyon National Park.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A thunderstorm complex near Phoenix on the evening of July 21st produced a strong outflow boundary that pushed through much of Pinal and Pima counties. Other scattered thunderstorms in Pima county produced locally damaging downburst winds.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A trained spotter reported near zero visibility in blowing dust at mile marker 214 along Interstate 10.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Several thunderstorms over Yavapai County produced large hail.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Three quarter inch diameter hail was reported in Cottonwood.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Low visibility due to dense blowing dust affected many areas betwee Eloy and Apache Junction during the evening hours. Thunderstorms and locally damaging winds were also reported.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A house at Third and Phoenix streets in Florence was struck by lightning, setting the house on fire.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Monsoon moisture fueled an outbreak of thunderstorms over the Mojave Desert.  Several storms produced high winds and flash flooding.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Two porches were blown over, and several homes had roof tiles damaged.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: High pressure aloft remained near the Four Corners region, resulting in a continued east to northeast flow aloft over southeast Arizona. Plentiful moisture again led to scattered afternoon and evening thunderstorms. With boundary layer moisture still somewhat drier, thunderstorms were again able to produce strong to severe downburst or microburst winds.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorm outflow winds blew down a power line at the intersection of Blacklidge and Campbell, and then also at the intersection of Speedway and 6th Avenue.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong ridge of high pressure aloft repositioned to southern New Mexico by the afternoon of July 8th. With the ridge to the east, a more favorable southeast flow for thunderstorms was established with an increased moisture supply across southeast Arizona. Scattered thunderstorms interacted with a relatively dry boundary layer to produce damaging wind gusts, but also produced enough rainfall to result in some flash flooding events.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorm winds downed several power poles near the Bonita school.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A push of monsoon moisture triggered thunderstorms over the Mojave Desert.  One storm produced large hail.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Quarter sized hail fell in Chloride.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Monsoon moisture fueled an outbreak of thunderstorms over the Mojave Desert.  Several storms produced high winds and flash flooding.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Roads were flooded near the intersection of Pierce Ferry Road and Stockton Hill Road.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A tornado was sighted over the central part of the Park by visiting tourists.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Tourists visiting the Petrified Forest National Park saw a tornado over the central portion of the park.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong ridge of high pressure was centered over the southern plains on July 10th.  A shortwave trough rotating around this upper high tracked from near Chihuahua, Mexico into southeast Arizona. This disturbance arrived at peak heating, encouraging the development of scattered thunderstorms. With a relatively dry boundary layer, the primary threat from thunderstorms was damaging wind gusts.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Law enforcement reported numerous tree fires started by lightning, centered near the intersection of Golf Links Road and Kolb Road.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A tornado was sighted in Doney Park northwest of Flagstaff.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Possible tornado reported by Coconino Sheriffs Office.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong ridge of high pressure aloft repositioned to southern New Mexico by the afternoon of July 8th. With the ridge to the east, a more favorable southeast flow for thunderstorms was established with an increased moisture supply across southeast Arizona. Scattered thunderstorms interacted with a relatively dry boundary layer to produce damaging wind gusts, but also produced enough rainfall to result in some flash flooding events.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Severe thunderstorm winds downed several trees, including one tree that was 60 feet tall in San Manuel. Thunderstorm winds also blew down some fencing in the area. Large tree branches were downed by the same thunderstorm in Mammoth.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong ridge of high pressure was centered over the southern plains on July 10th.  A shortwave trough rotating around this upper high tracked from near Chihuahua, Mexico into southeast Arizona. This disturbance arrived at peak heating, encouraging the development of scattered thunderstorms. With a relatively dry boundary layer, the primary threat from thunderstorms was damaging wind gusts.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Severe thunderstorm downburst winds blew off approximately one half of the roof of a tire shop at Golf Links Road and Kolb Road. The ceiling collapsed causing extensive damage. Downed power lines were reported near 20th and Craycroft Streets. Roof damage to a house was reported near Scarlett Street and Koralee Street. Several trees fell on cars parked at the Tucson Convention Center.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong ridge of high pressure was centered over the southern plains on July 10th.  A shortwave trough rotating around this upper high tracked from near Chihuahua, Mexico into southeast Arizona. This disturbance arrived at peak heating, encouraging the development of scattered thunderstorms. With a relatively dry boundary layer, the primary threat from thunderstorms was damaging wind gusts.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Lightning struck a tree near a restaurant parking lot, near the intersection of Wilmot Road and Tanque Verde Road. The tree caught fire and was destroyed.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: High pressure aloft remained near the Four Corners region, resulting in a continued east to northeast flow aloft over southeast Arizona. Plentiful moisture again led to scattered afternoon and evening thunderstorms. With boundary layer moisture still somewhat drier, thunderstorms were again able to produce strong to severe downburst or microburst winds.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Severe thunderstorm outflow winds estimated at 60 mph blew down a large tree near downtown Nogales.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An upper-level disturbance rotated into southeast Arizona, around a strong ridge of high pressure over the southern plains. As the disturbance moved northwest out of Sonora, Mexico, it interacted with a rich moisture supply to produce scattered thunderstorms across southeast Arizona.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Severe thunderstorm outflow winds downed power lines near the intersection of Valencia and Mark Rds.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An upper-level disturbance rotated into southeast Arizona, around a strong ridge of high pressure over the southern plains. As the disturbance moved northwest out of Sonora, Mexico, it interacted with a rich moisture supply to produce scattered thunderstorms across southeast Arizona.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorm winds estimated at 60 mph downed several power poles just north of Ajo.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: High pressure aloft shifted to western Arizona on July 20th, creating northwest flow aloft over southeast Arizona. Scattered afternoon thunderstorms developed over the region.  A relatively dry boundary layer encouraged the production of strong thunderstorm downburst winds.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Strong thunderstorm downburst winds downed 4 small trees.  Minor roofing damage was reported with missing roof tiles, and one street sign was blown down.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong high pressure aloft was centered over northern Arizona on July 17th, setting up a brisk northeast flow aloft over southeast Arizona. Afternoon thunderstorms that formed on the Mogollon Rim and the White Mountains northeast of Tucson were pushed southwest across southern Arizona. A relatively dry boundary layer led to several instances of damaging downburst winds, along with areas of blowing dust.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Strong thunderstorm winds created a dust storm, with the public reporting visibility of 40 feet in the blowing dust at Eloy.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong high pressure aloft was centered over northern Arizona on July 17th, setting up a brisk northeast flow aloft over southeast Arizona. Afternoon thunderstorms that formed on the Mogollon Rim and the White Mountains northeast of Tucson were pushed southwest across southern Arizona. A relatively dry boundary layer led to several instances of damaging downburst winds, along with areas of blowing dust.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Strong thunderstorm winds created widespread blowing dust in Safford, with a trained spotter reporting less than 1/4 mile visibility in the downtown area.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong ridge of high pressure was centered over the southern plains on July 10th.  A shortwave trough rotating around this upper high tracked from near Chihuahua, Mexico into southeast Arizona. This disturbance arrived at peak heating, encouraging the development of scattered thunderstorms. With a relatively dry boundary layer, the primary threat from thunderstorms was damaging wind gusts.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Law enforcement reported a ramada was blown down at the Mammoth swimming pool.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A tornado was sighted in Doney Park northwest of Flagstaff.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A tornado was reported by the public around Doney Park about 15 miles northeast of Flagstaff.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A brush fire burned more than 5,000 acres and at least four homes in a subdivision near Fort Huachuca's West Gate. One person suffered burn injuries related to the fire. Dry westerly flow brought dry conditions to Santa Cruz County, with winds gusting to near 25 mph.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Wildfire burned near 5,000 acres near Fort Huachuca in Santa Cruz County. The fire burned at least four homes and six private vehicles near the west gate entrance. One injury was reported due to burns from the fire.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: With high pressure aloft over the Four-Corners region...northeast flow aloft prevailed over Southeast Arizona. Thunderstorms developed over the higher terrain northeast of Tucson...and propogated southwestward. With the boundary layer still relatively dry...thunderstorms were able to aggregate and strengthen their outflow winds. Several severe wind gusts resulted.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Severe thunderstorm outflow winds tore a portion of the roofing material off a strip mall store near the intersection of Grant and Campbell. The debris from the roof blew into the parking lot, but resulted in no injuries. Winds from the same thunderstorm uprooted trees at the intersection of Pantano and 22nd Street, and damaged an awning on a mobile home. One woman and one child received minor injuries after a tree limb fell on them on the 1800 block of Mission Road. A large tree also fell onto two vehicles.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong ridge of high pressure aloft repositioned to southern New Mexico by the afternoon of July 8th. With the ridge to the east, a more favorable southeast flow for thunderstorms was established with an increased moisture supply across southeast Arizona. Scattered thunderstorms interacted with a relatively dry boundary layer to produce damaging wind gusts, but also produced enough rainfall to result in some flash flooding events.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Sheriff closed La Canada Road just south of Duval Mine Road due to flash flooding and flowing debris on the roadway.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: High pressure aloft shifted to northern New Mexico, provided light easterly flow to southeast Arizona. Thunderstorms that formed on the White Mountains and near the Mogollon Rim slowly moved southwest. With an unseasonably dry boundary layer, storms struggled to maintain themselves. As the thunderstorms collapsed, their outflow generated widespread areas of blowing dust.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Law enforcement reported visibility less than 1/2 mile due to blowing dust at San Simon. Visibility of 1/4 mile in blowing dust was observed on the Arizona Department of Transportation webcam along Interstate 10 near Bowie. The peak gust at the webcam site was 46 mph.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: High pressure aloft remained near the Four Corners region, resulting in a continued east to northeast flow aloft over southeast Arizona. Plentiful moisture again led to scattered afternoon and evening thunderstorms. With boundary layer moisture still somewhat drier, thunderstorms were again able to produce strong to severe downburst or microburst winds.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Severe thunderstorm downburst winds caused significant damage at Three Points. Several mobile homes and nearby sheds were either heavily damaged or destroyed.  A more substantial brick veneer building was also damaged, with varying degrees of roof damage reported to several homes in Three Points. Several large trees were uprooted completely. Winds from this severe thunderstorm were estimated to be near 85 mph. Three Points Fire reported one injury was received by flying glass, after winds blew out a house window.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An upper-level impulse passing through southeast Arizona contributed to some dry thunderstorms on the evening of June 9th. With the dry lower levels of the atmosphere, the showers and storms produced strong microburst winds.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A strong microburst wind gust of 70 mph snapped a 125-foot flag pole in half. The wind gust was recorded via a local television station's weather station at the RV park where the flag pole was located. No other damage was noted nearby.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Winds associated with thunderstorms caused areas of blowing dust along with spotty minor damage.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Trees were blown down in Queen Creek and winds were estimated at 60 mph at Southern and Dobson in Mesa. Other areas of the East Valley experieced strong and gusty winds.  In addition, winds reached 55 mph at Sky Harbor airport at 6:40 PM.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Winds from nearby thunderstorms reached over 50 mph late in evening. Blowing dust also accompanied these strong winds.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Peak winds reached 56 mph at Luke AFB.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: The lightning caused Pioneer Fire started on May 30 near Globe Arizona and burned through ponderosa pine, manzanita, and oak brush vegetation in steep, rough terrain.  The fire threatened multi-million dollar communication towers in the Pinal Mountains. Fire fighting efforts prevented the fire from crossing Pioneer Canyon below the communication towers.  The fire was at least 45 % contained by June 13th at around 1,300 acres with little additional spread.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The lightning cause Pioneer fire burned over 1,300 acres on the eastern side of the Pinal Mountains about 8 miles south of Globe. Total suppression cost was three million dollars.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: The lightning caused Pioneer Fire started on May 30 near Globe Arizona and burned through ponderosa pine, manzanita, and oak brush vegetation in steep, rough terrain.  The fire threatened multi-million dollar communication towers in the Pinal Mountains. Fire fighting efforts prevented the fire from crossing Pioneer Canyon below the communication towers.  The fire was at least 45 % contained by June 13th at around 1,300 acres with little additional spread.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The lightning caused Pioneer Fire burned about 1,300 acres on the eastern side of the Pinal Mountains (East Mountain) about 8 miles south of Globe. The total cost for suppression was three million dollars.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong surge of moisture from the Gulf of California resulted in scattered thunderstorms across southeast Arizona.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: AWOS measured a wind gust of 59 mph at Ryan Field at 5:55 PM MST.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: With high pressure aloft centered over New Mexico, moist southerly flow prevailed over southeast Arizona. Scattered thunderstorms developed in the afternoon, with a few producing strong downburst winds.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A trained spotter reported blowing dust, with visibility less than 1/8 mile between Eloy and Picacho. Winds were estimated to be near 50 mph.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Gusty winds from thunderstorms caused some damage and local flooding to parts of Yuma County.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Local flooding reported in Wellton. The heavy rain along Interstate 8 near Araby Road may have contributed to a two-vehicle collision.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Gusty winds from thunderstorms caused some damage and local flooding to parts of Yuma County.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Several power poles were downed due to strong thunderstorm winds.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: High pressure aloft shifted to northern New Mexico, provided light easterly flow to southeast Arizona. Thunderstorms that formed on the White Mountains and near the Mogollon Rim slowly moved southwest. With an unseasonably dry boundary layer, storms struggled to maintain themselves. As the thunderstorms collapsed, their outflow generated widespread areas of blowing dust.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Law enforcement reported 1/4 mile visibility in blowing dust at Guthrie, with strong winds in progress. A trained spotter reported a visibility of 50 feet in blowing dust, along Highway 75, 4 miles southeast of Guthrie. Law enforcement reported 50 feet visibility in blowing dust at the Graham County sheriff office.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A large complex or area of storms moved to the west and into Yuma late on Saturday afternoon.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorm winds created a huge dust storm that affected much of the Yuma area with near zero visibility. Wind speeds were estimated to be over 60 mph, with consderable damage to property. At least one home was damaged, with trees and power lines downed by strong winds. During the peak of the storm, 5,200 customers were without power. The Yuma airport recorded a peak gust of 48 mph just before 5 PM.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Very strong winds developed from thunderstorms over far southwest Maricopa County on Sunday evening. Considerable damage resulted as storms moved to the north.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: About 65 power poles were blown down in the Gila Bend area due to very strong microburst winds. About 2300 APS customers were left without power. The NWS storm survey indicated the straight line winds were generally from the southwest. One wind speed indicator near Gila Bend went off the chart at 100 mph. There was considerable damage to buildings and homes in the area. The airbase was also affected with several buildings dstroyed.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Visibility dropped to less than 1/4 mile in many areas on Sunday evening.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Near zero visibility was reported in many areas. In parts of Tempe and Mesa winds took down tree limbs and at least one large tree was uprooted.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Low visibility due to dense blowing dust affected many areas betwee Eloy and Apache Junction during the evening hours. Thunderstorms and locally damaging winds were also reported.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Numerous spotter reports of dense blowing dust with visibility at or below 1/4 mile. Winds of 51 mph were reported at the Casa Grande airport, with a visibility of only 1/2 mile. Strong winds in Arizona City pulled tiles from some roof tops and knocked trees over.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Low visibility due to dense blowing dust affected many areas betwee Eloy and Apache Junction during the evening hours. Thunderstorms and locally damaging winds were also reported.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Roof damage due to thunderstorm winds.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Severe thunderstorm winds resulted in a power outage in the Roosevelt Lakes estates area.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Power outage on Wednesday evening at the Frazier substation was due to very strong winds. Spotter reports included debrs blowing around due to the strong wind. Heavy rain was also reported, with .98 inch in 45 minutes at Roosevelt Dam.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong winds reported at Horseshoe Dam.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Measured winds of 61 mph at Horseshoe Dam.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Brief heavy rain affected parts of the Miami and Globe area.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Streets in a small area of Miami were closed early on Thursday morning due to the runoff from heavy rains late Wednesday.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong storm system and cold front moved across northern Arizona and produced strong to very strong winds.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Arizona Department of Transportation reported strong winds in Lupton with a peak wind gust of 59 MPH.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain from a thunderstorm caused flash flooding northwest of the community of Rough Rock.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain caused flash flooding along portions of Route 59 about 6 miles northwest of Rough Rock.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Southerly flow increased the moisture supply over Southeast Arizona. Scattered thunderstorms resulted in isolated microbursts yielding brief severe wind gusts.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: ASOS measured a wind gust of 62 mph at the Safford Airport.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorms developed across the western portions of the Phoenix area during the evening hours as outflows from earlier storms interacted with each other.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Microburst wind speed at Luke AFB was measured at 60 knots. Blowing dust was also reported resulting in 1/4 mile visibility.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A series of storms developed after dark across metro Phoenix and some produced heavy rain and strong damaging winds.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Strong winds reported at 107th Avenue and Camelback. Numerous trees uprooted by winds. Luke AFB recorded winds that gusted to 52 mph.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorms moved slowly across the area, dumping about an inch of rain in less than an hour.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Several reports of heavy rain in the Globe and Miami area. One of the heaviest amounts was 1.30 inches in about 50 minutes which resulted in minior flooding. Pea size hail was also reported at the time of the heavy rainfall. One spotter in Miami reported a wall of water coming towards him shortly after 7 PM.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong winds and heavy rain pounded parts of Gila County late in the afternoon.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Very heavy rain caused streets to flood and at least one landslide on the hill near Miami. The official observer in Globe had .97 inches in less than an hour. Strong winds and small hail accompanied the storms.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: High pressure aloft shifted to western Arizona on July 20th, creating northwest flow aloft over southeast Arizona. Scattered afternoon thunderstorms developed over the region.  A relatively dry boundary layer encouraged the production of strong thunderstorm downburst winds.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Severe thunderstorm winds downed 20 power poles near the intersection of Valencia Road and Ajo Way.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Showers and thunderstorms propagated off the mountains and into the Roosevelt area late in the afternoon Saturday.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain with thunderstorms struck Roosevelt late in the afternoon. One report included 1.47 inches in just 48 minutes at Roosevelt dam.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Showers and thunderstorms propagated off the mountains and into the Roosevelt area late in the afternoon Saturday.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Lake patrol at Roosevelt Lake reported a lightning strike that caused a brief power outage at the marina. A small shed was also damaged.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: With high pressure aloft still centered in northern New Mexico, easterly flow was maintained for southeast Arizona. The atmosphere was unseasonably dry with limited moisture available. Thunderstorms that initiated near the White Mountains remained rather weak, but their outflows generated areas of blowing dust as they propagated to the southwest.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A trained spotter reported visibility less than 50 feet in blowing dust along U.S. Highway 191 south of Safford. Wind gusts of 50-55 mph were reported by ASOS and trained spotters in the Safford area.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: High pressure aloft was centered near northwest New Mexico by the afternoon of August 21, while a cutoff low remained stationary west of San Diego. These two systems combined to return southeast flow to southeast Arizona. The resulting increase in moisture led to scattered strong to severe thunderstorms, with the primary threat being damaging winds.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: ASOS measured a 62 mph thunderstorm wind gust at Fort Huachuca.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A Pacific cold front helped lift deep subtropical moisture seeping north from the remnants of Hurricane Jimena, triggering severe storms and flash flooding in the Mojave Desert.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Golf ball size hail and high wind broke all the windows on the west side of the spotter's house, broke his weather station, and damaged his shortwave radio equipment.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A Pacific cold front helped lift deep subtropical moisture seeping north from the remnants of Hurricane Jimena, triggering severe storms and flash flooding in the Mojave Desert.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Seven mobile home trailers were blown over.  Several others suffered minor to moderate damage.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A Pacific cold front helped lift deep subtropical moisture seeping north from the remnants of Hurricane Jimena, triggering severe storms and flash flooding in the Mojave Desert.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Runoff and mud covered Primavera Loop and Primavera Rd. near Mohave Valley.  Two homes were destroyed, nine had moderate damage, and 16 had minor damage.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Brief heavy rain and some gusty winds moved slowly to the south late in the afternoon.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A wind gust of 56 mph from the north was reported by a spotter. A 300 gallon water tank was blown over.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A thunderstorm caused severe wind gusts in Winslow.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Winslow ASOS reported a 50 knot wind gust with a thunderstorm.  Police in Winslow reported strong winds from 120 PM MST through at least 138 PM MST.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A Pacific cold front helped lift deep subtropical moisture seeping north from the remnants of Hurricane Jimena, triggering severe storms and flash flooding in the Mojave Desert.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: This gust occurred at the Kingman Airport.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A Pacific cold front helped lift deep subtropical moisture seeping north from the remnants of Hurricane Jimena, triggering severe storms and flash flooding in the Mojave Desert.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Water, rocks and other debris covered several roads in Valle Vista.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A Pacific cold front helped lift deep subtropical moisture seeping north from the remnants of Hurricane Jimena, triggering severe storms and flash flooding in the Mojave Desert.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Several power poles along Bullhead City Parkway were snapped and blown down.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Winds were estimated at ovr 60 mph across the area.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Two power poles near McCartney and Cox roads were downed by thunderstorm winds.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong ridge of high pressure aloft repositioned to southern New Mexico by the afternoon of July 8th. With the ridge to the east, a more favorable southeast flow for thunderstorms was established with an increased moisture supply across southeast Arizona. Scattered thunderstorms interacted with a relatively dry boundary layer to produce damaging wind gusts, but also produced enough rainfall to result in some flash flooding events.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Swift water rescue was performed at Stone Avenue underpass near 6th Street.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong high pressure aloft was centered over northern Arizona on July 17th, setting up a brisk northeast flow aloft over southeast Arizona. Afternoon thunderstorms that formed on the Mogollon Rim and the White Mountains northeast of Tucson were pushed southwest across southern Arizona. A relatively dry boundary layer led to several instances of damaging downburst winds, along with areas of blowing dust.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Severe thunderstorm winds downed many power poles near the intersection of Tangerine Road and Thornydale Road. A NWS employee reported a large downed tree near the intersection of First Avenue and Orange Grove Road.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong ridge of high pressure aloft repositioned to southern New Mexico by the afternoon of July 8th. With the ridge to the east, a more favorable southeast flow for thunderstorms was established with an increased moisture supply across southeast Arizona. Scattered thunderstorms interacted with a relatively dry boundary layer to produce damaging wind gusts, but also produced enough rainfall to result in some flash flooding events.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A trained spotter reported 0.75 inch hail at San Manuel.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong ridge of high pressure aloft repositioned to southern New Mexico by the afternoon of July 8th. With the ridge to the east, a more favorable southeast flow for thunderstorms was established with an increased moisture supply across southeast Arizona. Scattered thunderstorms interacted with a relatively dry boundary layer to produce damaging wind gusts, but also produced enough rainfall to result in some flash flooding events.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorm winds estimated at 60 mph downed several power lines near the intersection of La Canada and Duval Mine Roads in Green Valley. The Green Valley newspaper reported that approximately one dozen power poles were damaged or destroyed. This resulted in a power loss to over 6000 people in the area.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong ridge of high pressure aloft repositioned to southern New Mexico by the afternoon of July 8th. With the ridge to the east, a more favorable southeast flow for thunderstorms was established with an increased moisture supply across southeast Arizona. Scattered thunderstorms interacted with a relatively dry boundary layer to produce damaging wind gusts, but also produced enough rainfall to result in some flash flooding events.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorm downdraft winds downed power poles near the intersection of Grande and St. Marys Roads, and 10 power poles were downed or damaged near the intersection of Irvington Road and Harrison Road.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A rich supply of moisture across southeast Arizona led to numerous showers and thunderstorms during the evening hours. A large complex of thunderstorms affected much of eastern Pima county, with many areas receiving significant rainfall.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Swift water rescue reported by broadcast media near the intersection of Bonanza and Tanque Verde.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong high pressure aloft dominated the southwestern states for most of the month. |July 2009 was the hottest month on record in Phoenix...wth a mean monthly temperature of 98.3F, which was 3.5F hotter than normal. The period from July 10 to  July 20 was the hottest in the Valley's climatological history, with an average maximum temperature of 112.7 degrees during those 10 days. Yuma was also very hot with a mean monthly temperature of  97.5F, or 3.8 degrees above normal.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Prolonged heat throughout much of the month of July contributed to 10 heat-caused deaths, according to the Maricopa County medical examiner. July 2009 was not only the hottest July on record but the hottest month of all-time in Phoenix. Records for Phoenix began in 1896. There were 15 days in July where highs reached 110 or hotter at Phoenix Sky Harbor airport.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A thunderstorm produced severe hail in the Heber-Overgaard area.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A thunderstorm produced nickel to quarter sized (0.88 to 1.00 inch diameter) hail in Overgaard.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A thunderstorm produced severe hail in the Heber-Overgaard area.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A spotter in Heber-Overgaard reported one inch of rain in just 30 minutes with dime to nickel sized hail. There was standing water in his yard.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A rich supply of moisture across southeast Arizona led to numerous showers and thunderstorms during the evening hours. A large complex of thunderstorms affected much of eastern Pima county, with many areas receiving significant rainfall.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Broadcast media reported that a swift water rescue was performed in Corona de Tucson.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong high pressure aloft was centered over northern Arizona on July 17th, setting up a brisk northeast flow aloft over southeast Arizona. Afternoon thunderstorms that formed on the Mogollon Rim and the White Mountains northeast of Tucson were pushed southwest across southern Arizona. A relatively dry boundary layer led to several instances of damaging downburst winds, along with areas of blowing dust.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A trained spotter reported many trees were downed in the central part of Safford. ASOS measured a 63 mph thunderstorm wind gust at the Safford Regional Airport.|Amateur radio operators reported numerous power poles down near Safford.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Abundant moisture and weak winds aloft led to scattered slow moving thunderstorms across southeast Arizona. The primary threats from the storms were heavy rainfall and strong downburst winds.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Strong thunderstorm downburst winds downed or snapped off 18 power poles in Green Valley. One of the power poles landed on a house.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A thunderstorm complex near Phoenix on the evening of July 21st produced a strong outflow boundary that pushed through much of Pinal and Pima counties. Other scattered thunderstorms in Pima county produced locally damaging downburst winds.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Severe thunderstorm downburst winds downed several power poles and 10 power lines west of Sells. This caused a power disruption to the Papago Farms, Pisinimo, and Hickiwan areas.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Scattered afternoon and evening thunderstorms developed across much of southern Arizona. There were no severe weather reports in southeast Arizona, but storms did produce frequent lightning.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Lightning struck a porch of a mobile home in Picture Rocks, creating a fire. The fire spread into the home, causing damage.  One person suffered minor burn injuries to his hands and wrists while attempting to extinguish the fire.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A thunderstorm produced very strong wind gusts, heavy rain, and hail in Globe.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A thunderstorm over Globe produced wind gusts to 57 MPH on the north side of town.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A moist atmosphere across southeast Arizona fueled scattered afternoon and evening thunderstorms. Some cooling of temperatures in the middle levels of the atmosphere allowed for greater instability, and some storms produced small hail and strong to severe downburst winds.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A trained spotter measured a thunderstorm wind gust of 62 mph.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: High pressure aloft remained centered near northwest New Mexico on September 8th, resulting in a southeast flow that maintained a moisture supply across southeast Arizona.  With weak winds aloft, storms moved slowly, producing locally heavy rainfall and locally strong downburst winds. Outflow boundaries from storms encouraged further development into the evening hours.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Scattered thunderstorms produced locally heavy rainfall near the intersection of State Highway 80 and Old Divide Road, approximately 2 miles northwest of Bisbee.  Rainfall was not excessively heavy, with radar estimates of near 1.25 inch. However, an earlier fire burned hillsides in the area in May 2009, leaving the landscape prone to flash flooding.  Flooding and debris flows destroyed portions of the road and surrounding culverts, and forced the closure of Old Divide Road.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Several thunderstorms produced heavy rain and hail over the Heber-Overgaard area.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorms produced up to 2.50 inches of rain in just over two hours.  The heavy rain clogged a drainage ditch, flooded a yard, and flowed into a basement.

CZ_NAME: LITTLE COLORADO RIVER VALLEY IN COCONINO COUNTY
EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong cold front brought strong winds to the Little Colorado River Valley.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Arizona Department of Transportation weather station at Two Guns recorded sustained winds of 40 MPH or higher from 11 AM through 5 PM.  The highest sustained wind was 46 MPH around 5 PM.  The highest measured wind gust was 65 MPH.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A thunderstorm produced very heavy rain that caused flash flooding and damage to the Tlaquepaque area of Sedona.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain,  with rates up to 4 inches in an hour,  fell on the Red Rocks on the northwest side of Sedona.  With virtually no infiltration,  the water quickly flowed into normally dry channels and washes.  There were reports of numerous flooded homes, widespread street flooding, over flowing stream beds,  large boulders washed into streets, eroded paved roads, and cars moved and stacked by flood water.  A 4 foot wall of water swept through the Los Abrigados Resort parking lot and moved parked cars.  One normally dry gully filled with 6 feet of rushing water. Several people were rescued after their cars started floating in the rapidly raising water in parking lots. City officials estimated that clean on public roads and drainage would cost  over $600,000.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong cold front brought strong winds to the Little Colorado River Valley.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: High winds knocked down tree limbs and power lines in Prescott,  Groom Creek, and Walker.  As many as 6,300 customers lost power 5 to 6 times.  The downed power lines also caused a several small grass fires. Cable and phone lines were also knocked out.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Several thunderstorms produced heavy rain and hail over the Heber-Overgaard area.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorms also produced hail from pea to not quite dime/nickel sized (1/4 to  under 7/8 inch diameter).  The hail covered the ground and was piled two inches deep on a deck.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong cold front produced strong winds across northern Arizona.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A strong cold front produced strong winds across northern Arizona.  Limestone Canyon, Tuba City,  Show Low, and Winslow all reported wind gusts in the lower 50 MPH range.  Window Rock reported a peak wind gust of 58 MPH.

CZ_NAME: LITTLE COLORADO RIVER VALLEY IN NAVAJO COUNTY
EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong cold front brought strong winds to the Little Colorado River Valley.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Winslow ASOS has sustained winds above 32 MPH with estimated gusts above 58 MPH for much of the afternoon. The highest measured wind gust was 60 MPH.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A low pressure center near the for corners brought cold and snowy conditions to the Flagstaff area.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A departing low pressure center brought snow showers and cold conditions to the Flagstaff area during the afternoon and early evening.  These conditions lead to icy roads and few dozen car wrecks. The Department of Public Safety reported 11 collisions,  Coconino County Sheriff's Office reported 7 traffic accidents with injuries, and the Flagstaff Police Department reported 14 traffic accidents.  A parked DPS patrol car was hit and totaled by a truck that slid on the ice on I-40 just west of Flagstaff. The officer was out of his vehicle investigating a single vehicle roll over and was not hurt.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An unseasonably strong and cold low pressure system entered northwest Arizona on October 27.  As this storm system and its associated strong cold front approached southeast Arizona, strengthening pressure gradients created widespread winds and blowing dust.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Strong west and southwest winds continued well into the evening hours as a strong cold front passed through southeast Arizona. A trained spotter measured a wind gust of 60 mph at Bisbee in Cochise county. The spotter noted moderate broken tree branches at the time, along with flying debris and some minor roof damage. ASOS at Fort Huachuca measured a wind gust of 62 mph.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An unseasonably strong and cold low pressure system entered northwest Arizona on October 27.  As this storm system and its associated strong cold front approached southeast Arizona, strengthening pressure gradients created widespread winds and blowing dust.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Strong winds associated with a passing cold front created blowing dust across Cochise county into the overnight hours. Blowing dust created limited visibility on Interstate 10, which led to a three vehicle collision about 20 miles east of Willcox. A commercial truck entered the dust storm and slowed down due to the low visibility. A freight truck collided with the commercial truck from behind, before the freight truck was struck by a bus from behind as well. Three people from the three vehicles suffered injuries.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A winter storm moving toward Arizona caused several traffic accidents on I-17.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The early stages of an approaching winter storm caused a bridge to ice up on I-17 near Munds Park.  A Department of Public Safety officer was stopped to investigate a single van roll over.  The van had been traveling northbound, slid on the ice,  and crossed over the median into the southbound lane.  A northbound semi slid on the bridge,  crossed the median, and stuck the officer and the van.  The officer was pinned under a third vehicle that had stopped to avoid being hit by the semi.  People from other stopped vehicles were able to lift the vehicle off the officer,  free him, and call for help.  He was taken to a local hospital where he was listed in critical but stable condition.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A fast moving trough resulted in strong winds across Southeast Arizona with blowing dust on December 22nd along with heavy snow in the higher elevations of Northern Greenlee County.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The SNOTEL at Hannagan Meadow recorded 11 inches of snow in less than 12 hours from a fast moving storm.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Lightning started the Wildhorse Fire about 16 miles north of Williams and the Red Fire about one mile south of Red Mountain on July 21.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Lightning started the Wildhorse Fire about 16 miles north of Williams.  It burned about 1,149 acres by the end of July.  It then became part of the Wildhorse Complex with the Red Fire.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Lightning started the Wildhorse Fire about 16 miles north of Williams and the Red Fire about one mile south of Red Mountain on July 21.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Lightning started the Red Fire about one mile south of Red Mountain.  It burned about 130 acres before it became part of the Wildhorse Complex.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorms and locally heavy rain resulted in damage to roads and buildings in Tacna and Wellton.  Very heavy rain also affected parts of the city of Yuma and Quartzsite in the afternoon.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain hit Tacna with one inch in 30 minutes. Minor street flooding was reported in town, while roads Avenue 16E in the Dome Valley was washed out. At least one business in Wellton was damaged by flooding. The official amount at the Yuma airport was 1.62 inches.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorms and locally heavy rain resulted in damage to roads and buildings in Tacna and Wellton.  Very heavy rain also affected parts of the city of Yuma and Quartzsite in the afternoon.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Lightning struck a home near highway 95 and 5E, resulting in a fire. A haystack caught fire at Avenue 30E and County 8th Street after it was struck by lightning.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorms and locally heavy rain resulted in damage to roads and buildings in Tacna and Wellton.  Very heavy rain also affected parts of the city of Yuma and Quartzsite in the afternoon.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Power outage reported by Wellton Irrigation District lasted about 4 hours. Dome had a power outage beginning at 2:30 pm, lasting about 3 hours. Trees were uprooted and  power poles were damaged.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An unseasonably strong and cold low pressure system entered northwest Arizona on October 27.  As this storm system and its associated strong cold front approached southeast Arizona, strengthening pressure gradients created widespread winds and blowing dust.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Strong southwest winds ahead of an approaching cold front produced blowing dust across Pinal and Pima counties. Blowing dust produced zero visibility on Interstate 10 at the Pima/Pinal county line.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong thunderstorm produced heavy rain in the town to Globe.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A strong thunderstorm produced as much as 1.30 inches of rain in just 50 minutes across Globe which resulted in minior flooding. Pea size hail was also reported at the time of the heavy rainfall. One spotter in Miami reported a wall of water coming towards him shortly after 7 PM.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Severe thunderstorm winds resulted in a power outage in the Roosevelt Lakes estates area.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Power outage on Wednesday evening at the Frazier substation was due to very strong winds. Spotter reports included debrs blowing around due to the strong wind. Heavy rain was also reported, with .98 inch in 45 minutes at Roosevelt Dam.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Lightning stuck one house and also knocked out power to street lights.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Lightning stuck one house and also knocked out power to street lights.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A thunderstorm created severe winds in St Johns.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The winds from a thunderstorm gusted to 64 MPH at the St Johns ASOS.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: High pressure aloft remained centered near northwest New Mexico on September 8th, resulting in a southeast flow that maintained a moisture supply across southeast Arizona.  With weak winds aloft, storms moved slowly, producing locally heavy rainfall and locally strong downburst winds. Outflow boundaries from storms encouraged further development into the evening hours.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorm microburst winds estimated at 60 mph downed several trees near the intersection of Lambert Lane and La Canada Drive in Oro Valley. The trees ranged in diameter from 5 to 10 inches. Some minor roof tile damage was also reported.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An unseasonably strong and cold low pressure system entered northwest Arizona on October 27.  As this storm system and its associated strong cold front approached southeast Arizona, strengthening pressure gradients created widespread winds and blowing dust.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Strong southwest winds ahead of an approaching cold front produced blowing dust across southeast Pinal county. Blowing dust produced zero visibility on Interstate 10 near Red Rock.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: High pressure aloft was centered near northwest New Mexico by the afternoon of August 21, while a cutoff low remained stationary west of San Diego. These two systems combined to return southeast flow to southeast Arizona. The resulting increase in moisture led to scattered strong to severe thunderstorms, with the primary threat being damaging winds.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Severe thunderstorm winds estimated at 60 mph downed two large trees near the |intersection of La Cholla Boulevard and River Road.  These trees were tall enough to pull down a power line on the opposite end of the street. Another large tree near the intersection of Cortaro Farms Road and Camino de Oeste was downed by the thunderstorm winds, falling through a fence and into a swimming pool.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Moist southerly flow continued on August 23rd, with a high pressure ridge centered over West Texas, and a trough of low pressure over southern California. Disturbances embedded in the southerly flow enhanced thunderstorm development, resulting in both heavy rain and microburst potential.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Severe thunderstorm microburst winds caused significant damage to mobile homes at Mescal.  At least two mobile homes suffered roof damage or roof collapse. Several awnings on the mobile homes were destroyed. Thunderstorm winds were estimated to be near 70 mph.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Winds increased ahead of a cold front and gusted to over 40 mph at times across parts of South Central Arizona.  Low visibility was due to blowing dust and sand.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Blowing dust started to reduce the visibility around milepost 175 to 218, though it was heaviest near milepost 200 in the Eloy and Casa Grande area, where there was a good amount of loose dirt in the area.  The visibility was down to less than a quarter mile at times.  A spotter reported 1/4 to1/2 mile visibility north of Florence at 2 pm. Winds reached 35 mph at the nearby Casa Grande airport.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A thunderstorm produced heavy rain in Prescott.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain in Prescott caused street flooding.  Highway 69 and Emerald Trail were closed due to high water.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A series of storms developed after dark across metro Phoenix and some produced heavy rain and strong damaging winds.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Lightning struck a home in north Phoenix, knocking out many electrical appliances. However, no fire started from this lightning strke.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A Pacific cold front helped lift deep subtropical moisture seeping north from the remnants of Hurricane Jimena, triggering severe storms and flash flooding in the Mojave Desert.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: High wind and golf ball size hail broke all the windows on the west side of the spotter's house, broke his weather station, and damaged his shortwave radio equipment.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A thunderstorm produced very strong wind gusts, heavy rain, and hail in Globe.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Very heavy rain caused landslides on the hills around Globe and Miami. The official observer in Globe had 0.97 inches in less than an hour.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A thunderstorm produced very strong wind gusts, heavy rain, and hail in Globe.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Spotters reported flash flooding and land slides in and around the Globe-Miami area.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorms developed over the mountains and propagated toward the deserts during the early evening hours.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The loal sheriffs office reported a water rescue after 2 vehicles were stranded in a flooded wash. Water was about 3 feet deep near Signal Butte road and Southern Avenue. One spotter reported a downpour of 1/2 inch in just 15 minutes.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorms and locally heavy rain resulted in damage to roads and buildings in Tacna and Wellton.  Very heavy rain also affected parts of the city of Yuma and Quartzsite in the afternoon.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Washes were running around Quartzsite with street flooding in town. Route 95 south of town was closed due to the flooding.  A car rolled over in the flooded area.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong ridge of high pressure aloft repositioned to southern New Mexico by the afternoon of July 8th. With the ridge to the east, a more favorable southeast flow for thunderstorms was established with an increased moisture supply across southeast Arizona. Scattered thunderstorms interacted with a relatively dry boundary layer to produce damaging wind gusts, but also produced enough rainfall to result in some flash flooding events.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy rainfall flooded several roadways between Bonita and Klondyke.
